privileged_info_summary: The rectangle_stroke task is a GIMP image editing task using real landscape images from Unsplash (800x533 pixels). The task requires 8 manual steps to create a rectangular stroke outline: selecting the Rectangle Select tool (R), creating a selection (50-70% of image size), setting foreground color, applying stroke via Edit→Stroke Selection menu, and removing selection (Ctrl+Shift+A). The verifier uses OpenCV Canny edge detection with thresholds 50-150 and Hough Line Transform to detect rectangular patterns, with fallback projection analysis. All GIMP feature claims (keyboard shortcuts R, Ctrl+Shift+A, menu paths) are verified against official GIMP documentation. The 120-second timeout is plausible for the task complexity.
